name: Swordfish Ravioli with Olive Pesto
recipeIngredient: 1/2 Pound cooked swordfish (from Involtini or Paillard recipe)
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 6 quarts water to boil and add 2 tablespoons salt.
2. Roughly chop swordfish and place in a mixing bowl. Add tomato sauce, scallions, and basil; stir well to combine.
3. Roll pasta out to thinnest setting on the pasta machine and cut into eight 5 by 5 squares. Divide swordfish mixture among four squares and place remaining four squares on each one.
4. Carefully press the edges of the pasta together to form large square packages of ravioli.
5. Place ravioli in boiling water; lower heat to a high simmer and cook for four to five minutes, or until the pasta is tender and filling is hot.
6. Meanwhile, place olive oil and butter in a 12 to 14-inch saute pan over medium heat. Cook until butter is bubbling; add black olive paste. Let pan sit off heat until ravioli are done.
7. Carefully remove ravioli with a large slotted spoon and gently place all four in the pan with olive paste mixture.
8. Put pan over medium heat; gently shake to cover ravioli, scallions, and serve immediately.
Yield: 4 servings
